overview: This Modern mill deck aims to win by exhausting the opponent's library before they can reduce your life total to zero. It relies on a core of eight crabs for consistent milling, supported by a robust suite of removal, counterspells, and card advantage engines to survive aggressive strategies and close out games.
win_conditions: Reduce opponent's library to zero cards
Force opponent to draw from an empty library

keep_criteria: Hands with Drown in the Loch or Crypt Incursion against Living End
Keep hands with 2-3 lands, at least one early mill engine (Hedron Crab or Tasha's Hideous Laughter), and a discard spell (Thoughtseize/Duress) if you have a land drop.
Keep hands with Fractured Sanity if you can reach 5+ cards in your graveyard quickly.
mulligan_criteria: Hands lacking early land drops to follow a crab
Hands without interactive spells against fast decks
Mulligan hands with 0-1 lands, multiple copies of the same low-impact spell, or hands lacking early mill engines or disruption.

examples.reason: Allows leading with a turn 1 crab and having removal to stay alive, aligning with the advice to play early crabs and keep interactive spells.

early_game: Play a crab on turn one to establish early pressure and landfall triggers.
Use Fatal Push and Drown in the Loch to remove immediate threats and survive.
Fetch lands to double-dip on landfall and find shock lands.
Turn 1: Play a land, cast Hedron Crab or Tasha's Hideous Laughter.
If you have acceleration (Lotus Petal, Chrome Mox, Arcane Signet), use it to cast a mill creature or discard on turn 1.
Turn 2: Cast a second mill engine or use discard to remove a key threat.
Sequence lands to ensure you can cast your first two mill pieces by turn 2.
If you have Fractured Sanity, hold it for when your graveyard has 5+ cards.
mid_game: Execute the mill package with Fractured Sanity, Tasha's Hideous Laughter, and Archive Trap.
Generate card advantage with Visions of Beyond and Jace, the Perfected Mind to snowball mill effects.
Use Consign to Memory and Surgical Extraction to disrupt opponent strategies and remove hate cards.
Preserve your graveyard for Fractured Sanity and recursion.
Don't overcommit creatures if you can mill from your hand.
Use discard to protect your mill engines or remove key threats.
Manage mana to ensure you can cast Fractured Sanity at instant speed when your graveyard is large.
Commit mill creatures early to apply pressure and protect them with bounce or protection if available.
Hold back if the opponent has a clear answer (e.g., Rest in Peace, Surgical Extraction) and you can't protect your graveyard.
late_game: Close out games with Jace's -X ability or force draws with Cephalid Coliseum.
Utilize Shelldock Isle to cast hideaway spells cheaply.
Continue milling until the opponent runs out of cards.
Fractured Sanity cast from hand or graveyard when your graveyard has 10+ cards.
Aim to reduce opponent's library to 0 while keeping yours above 0.
Go all-in when you have 2+ mill engines on board, your graveyard is large enough for a lethal Fractured Sanity, or the opponent is below 5 cards in library.
Don't hold back if you can win this turn.

key_cards.name: Hedron Crab
key_cards.notes: Blocks aggressive creatures and triggers landfall with fetch lands.

key_cards.name: Ruin Crab
key_cards.notes: Blocks aggressive creatures and triggers landfall with fetch lands.

key_cards.name: Fractured Sanity
key_cards.notes: Flexible spell that can mill a large chunk or cycle for card advantage with no downsides.

key_cards.name: Tasha's Hideous Laughter
key_cards.notes: High variance mill that exiles cards, bypassing graveyard synergies like Murktide.

key_cards.name: Archive Trap
key_cards.notes: Auto-include in fetch-heavy formats for efficient milling.

key_cards.name: Visions of Beyond
key_cards.notes: Refills hand cheaply to snowball mill effects.

key_cards.name: Jace, the Perfected Mind
key_cards.notes: Provides card draw, mill, protection, and closes games with its -X ability.

key_cards.name: Fatal Push
key_cards.notes: Critical tool to stay alive; kills almost anything relevant except Murktide Regent.

key_cards.name: Drown in the Loch
key_cards.notes: Catch-all spell to prevent resolution or remove resolved creatures.

key_cards.name: Consign to Memory
key_cards.notes: Handles evoke, storm, Eldrazi, and opposing countermagic via replicate.

key_cards.name: Surgical Extraction
key_cards.notes: Exiles key milled cards or hate cards from the graveyard.

key_cards.name: Field of Ruin
key_cards.notes: Forces library searches to reduce Archive Trap cost and disrupts nonbasic-heavy decks.

key_cards.name: Oboro, Palace in the Clouds
key_cards.notes: Tech land that synergizes perfectly with a deck running eight crabs.

key_cards.name: Shelldock Isle
key_cards.notes: Enables cheap hideaway spells in the mid-to-late game.

key_cards.name: Cephalid Coliseum
key_cards.notes: Provides card draw or forces opponents to draw three cards to wrap up the mill plan.

sequencing_tips: Lead with a turn 1 crab unless facing heavy removal, then wait for a land drop.
Do not tap out; play mill spells at instant speed to keep removal and counters available for lethal threats.
Immediately remove hate cards like Endurance from the graveyard using Extirpate to avoid free recasts.
Prioritize killing early threats like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer or Murktide Regent before focusing on milling.
Play around opposing countermagic by sequencing mill spells carefully.
Common mistake: Overcommitting to attacking with creatures instead of milling.
Remember, mill wins the game, not combat.
Another mistake: Not tracking the opponent's library size.
Use Fractured Sanity when you can win.
Watch out for Leyline of the Void or Rest in Peace
have a plan B (like direct damage or mill creatures that don't rely on the graveyard).

matchups.advice: Block with crabs, remove Eidolon of the Great Revel immediately, and use Surgical Extraction/Extirpate to guess and exile key damage spells.
Disrupt their game plan with Surgical Extraction and Extirpate, and lean on Consign to Memory to counter the storm trigger off Grapeshot.
Kill early threats, mill fast while playing around counters, and resolve Ensnaring Bridge post-board to stall their attackers.
Exile undying creatures with Surgical Extraction and Extirpate on the stack or in the graveyard, mill normally, and watch for Endurance.
Sideboard in board wipes like Engineered Explosives and Toxic Deluge to slow them down, and ditch slow cards like Surgical Extraction and Visions of Beyond.
Mulligan for Drown in the Loch or Crypt Incursion, aim to mill and exile Living End, and stall big creatures with Ensnaring Bridge.
Be fast, kill their crabs quickly using fetch land triggers, sideboard in Leyline of Sanctity, and use hand disruption to slow them down.

Use discard to remove counterspells. Cast mill creatures in response to their counterspells. Use Fractured Sanity from the graveyard to bypass counters. Consider Spell Pierce or Negate to protect your own spells.
Use discard to remove key ramp pieces. Mill them out before they can cast their big spells. If they have a fast combo finisher, prioritize mill over removal.
Early discard to remove threats. Use Fatal Blow/Fatal Push to clear board. Mill quickly to race. Don't overextend creatures; prioritize graveyard size for Fractured Sanity.
Apply pressure with mill creatures. Use discard to remove key answers. Mill through their library while they try to stabilize. Fractured Sanity can win instantly if their library is low.

Win the graveyard war. Use discard to remove their mill engines. Prioritize casting Fractured Sanity when your graveyard is larger. Use Cryptbreaker to recur Tasha's or Hedron Crab. Control the board with discard and removal.

common_threats: Watch for graveyard hate (Rest in Peace, Surgical Extraction, Leyline of the Void), sweepers (Wrath of God, Anger of the Gods), and combo win conditions.

overview_evidence.evidence_span: The strategy is simple: Reduce your opponents’ library to zero cards before they kill you.

win_conditions_evidence.evidence_span: If a player runs out of cards in their library and must draw a card, they lose the game.

sequencing_tips_evidence.evidence_span: It’s usually good to lead with a turn 1 crab because your opponent could have difficulty dealing with it.
You run a mix of mill, removal, and counters, so don't tap out and try to play your mill spells at instant speed.
Hate cards like Endurance are good to immediately remove from your opponent's graveyard with Extirpate.
answer the threats that immediately kill you, like Colossus Hammer or Murktide Regent , rather than the ones that are mostly just annoying.
mill them as fast as you can by playing around counters as much as you can.

common_threats_evidence.evidence_span: These little guys can block some of the most aggressive creatures in Modern like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer or Goblin Guide pretty well.
answer the threats that immediately kill you, like Colossus Hammer or Murktide Regent
Hate cards like Endurance are good to immediately remove from your opponent's graveyard
One card you may want to remove as soon as possible is Eidolon of the Great Revel
handle the namesake Ruby Medallion , it counters the actual storm trigger off Grapeshot
assemble their combo with its signature card Yawgmoth, Thran Physician along with their undying creatures
Boros Energy’s a terrifyingly fast deck even in a post- Amped Raptor Modern
